author: Dr. Lizard
title: What percentage of TP53-mutated MDS cases are classified discrepantly between ICC and WHO-5?
answer: Approximately 67.5% of TP53-mutated cases receive discrepant classifications between ICC and WHO-5 systems.
detail: The main discrepancy stems from different VAF thresholds and allelic status requirements between the systems.
ICC requires VAF at least 10% while WHO-5 has no specified VAF threshold for biallelic cases.
Complex karyotype serves as multi-hit equivalent in ICC but not in WHO-5 classification system.
Cases with 10-19% blasts are classified as MDS/AML in ICC but MDS-biTP53 in WHO-5.
releaseNote: This card highlights the discrepancy in classification of TP53-mutated MDS cases between the International Consensus Criteria (ICC) and the World Health Organization (WHO) classification system, with approximately 67.5% of cases receiving discrepant classifications.
